---
title: "Projects"
---

Below are selected projects from my graduate work at Emory University. Click the links to explore the code and full analysis.

<div class="section-label"><span>Graduate Projects</span></div>

<div class="projects-grid">

<div class="project-card">
<h3>Heart Disease Risk Analysis</h3>
<p>Investigated risk factors associated with heart disease using a large clinical dataset. Applied logistic regression and machine learning models to identify key predictors such as age, cholesterol, and blood pressure. Results were visualized in an interactive report to support evidence-based understanding of cardiovascular health patterns in the general population.</p>
<div>
<span class="tag">R</span>
<span class="tag">Logistic Regression</span>
<span class="tag">Data Visualization</span>
<span class="tag">Public Health</span>
</div>
<br>
<a class="btn-link" href="https://github.com/xian703/550_Final_Project_Heart_Disease" target="_blank">
<i class="fab fa-github"></i> View on GitHub
</a>
</div>

<div class="project-card">
<h3>Reproducible Data Analysis Pipeline</h3>
<p>Built a fully reproducible data analysis pipeline using R Markdown, covering data cleaning, exploratory analysis, and statistical modeling. The project emphasized best practices in computational reproducibility, including version control and automated report generation — skills essential for transparent and replicable public health research.</p>
<div>
<span class="tag">R</span>
<span class="tag">R Markdown</span>
<span class="tag">Reproducible Research</span>
<span class="tag">Data Wrangling</span>
</div>
<br>
<a class="btn-link" href="https://github.com/xian703/Midterm_project_Data550" target="_blank">
<i class="fab fa-github"></i> View on GitHub
</a>
</div>

</div>
